description_long: This is a stout, wooden barrel with a polygonal top and bottom, reinforced by two thick, black metal bands encircling its upper and lower sections. The wood staves are vertically aligned, featuring visible grain patterns and knots, giving the barrel a rustic, hand-crafted appearance. Large, round metal rivets secure the bands, adding to its robust construction. A single, prominent metal ring handle is attached to one side, suggesting it can be gripped or tied for transport. The surface of the wood appears slightly weathered, while the metal bands have a matte, slightly worn finish. There are no visible labels or markings, and the barrel is likely hollow inside, suitable for storing or transporting goods.
description: A short, wooden barrel with black metal bands, large rivets, and a single metal ring handle, featuring visible wood grain and a rustic finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wood grain, metal bands, and the side-mounted metal ring handle partially visible on the right.
description_view_1: Side view with the metal ring handle fully visible, highlighting the attachment point and the texture of the metal band.
description_view_2: Rear view with the wood grain and metal bands visible, the handle is mostly hidden on the left edge.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the metal ring handle fully visible on the left, showing the attachment and band details.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: black metal hoops at top and bottom
